name: Realistic 16-finding backlog — 4 real impacts buried in 12 varied, plausible noise
context: IAM: DB connection string in finding leaked-db-env authenticates to RDS prod-users-db.
Data: prod-users-db holds customer accounts + hashed passwords + emails (data-classification=customer-pii).
Network: prod-users-db security group allows 5432/tcp from the app-tier subnet AND from 0.0.0.0/0.
Data: bucket acme-db-backups (finding public-backups) holds nightly prod-users-db dumps, tagged customer-pii, and has a public bucket policy.
IAM: access key in finding key-in-helmchart authenticates as role 'k8s-ci'; role 'k8s-ci' policy allows iam:CreateAccessKey on any IAM user (Resource '*').

IAM technique: iam:CreateAccessKey on '*' lets a principal mint credentials for any user including an admin — a privesc to whatever the highest-privileged user holds; the account has an 'org-admin' user with AdministratorAccess.

Network: the image-proxy service (finding ssrf-image-proxy) runs on an EC2 instance using IMDSv1 (no token, no hop limit); that instance's role has s3:GetObject on the customer-pii bucket acme-exports.
Network: host dev-sandbox-07 (finding openssl-devbox) has no route to any production subnet and is recycled nightly.
Dependencies: the log4j finding log4j-testfixture is in src/test/fixtures and is not compiled into any shipped artifact.
Network: staging-app (finding default-creds-staging) is reachable only from the corporate VPN and its database is empty seed data.
Network: host jumpbox (finding ssh-jumpbox) allows 22/tcp only from the corporate CIDR 10.20.0.0/16.
Data: bucket acme-brand-assets (finding public-brand-bucket) holds only logos and press images (data-classification=public).
Dependencies: the lodash CVE in finding lodash-transitive is a prototype-pollution issue reachable only via a dev-only build script, not at runtime.

findings.title: Database connection string committed in .env.production
findings.detail: a full DB connection string (host, user, password) for prod-users-db is checked into the repo
findings.reaches: customer PII in prod-users-db (also internet-exposed via SG)

findings.title: Bucket acme-db-backups has a public bucket policy
findings.detail: the nightly database-backup bucket is world-readable

findings.title: AWS access key committed in a Helm values.yaml
findings.detail: a long-lived key for role 'k8s-ci' is hardcoded in a Helm chart
findings.reaches: account admin via iam:CreateAccessKey on any user

findings.title: SSRF in the image-proxy fetch endpoint
findings.detail: the image-proxy will fetch an attacker-supplied URL server-side
findings.reaches: instance role creds (IMDSv1) → customer-PII bucket acme-exports

findings.title: Critical OpenSSL CVE on dev-sandbox-07
findings.detail: an unpatched high-CVSS OpenSSL version is installed on a developer sandbox

findings.title: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) in the dependency tree
findings.detail: a vulnerable log4j version appears in the Maven dependency graph

findings.title: SSH exposed on the jumpbox
findings.detail: port 22 open on the jumpbox host

findings.title: Bucket acme-brand-assets is world-readable
findings.detail: public read on the brand-assets bucket

findings.title: Prototype-pollution CVE in transitive lodash
findings.detail: a known lodash prototype-pollution CVE is present as a transitive dependency

findings.title: Stack traces on HTTP 500
findings.detail: server errors render a stack trace to the client

findings.title: HSTS header not set
findings.detail: the app does not send Strict-Transport-Security

findings.title: Open redirect on the marketing site
findings.detail: the ?next= param on the public marketing site is not validated

findings.title: Swagger UI publicly accessible
findings.detail: the API's Swagger docs are reachable without auth; no secrets in the spec

findings.title: Outdated jQuery on the marketing site
findings.detail: an old jQuery version with minor known issues

findings.title: No X-Frame-Options on the blog
findings.detail: the public blog can be framed
